Chordata > Actinopterygii > Carangiformes > Toxotoidei > Toxotidae

Banded archerfish

Toxotes jaculatrix

Updated Oct 2020

Where seen?

This active fish is often seen in groups under jetties, bridges in streams near mangroves. There is usually large groups of really large fat ones under the main bridge at Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve.

Features

To about 20cm long, those seen about 7-10cm. 4-5 broad black vertical bands that taper towards the bottom of the body. More about the archerfishes’ infamous ability to shoot down insects.

Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve, Feb 11
Sungei Buloh Wetland Reserve, Feb 05

Human uses: Sometimes sold fresh in markets. Also taken for the live aquarium trade.

Banded archerfishes on Singapore shores

On wildsingapore flickr
